The worst is initials, JL, right? Because that we sat in and made it up. And the best is all the identification that might matter to the people who are seeing it. So the details about the person and then the details of the story. Unless you are 146 character limited, you don't wanna leave out that it started on a dark and stormy night. You want those details. Often you want your testimonials to erase specific objections. So you should know what the five or six reasons are that stop people from buying your thing, right? And your testimonials can erase those five or six objections. And then last, I would say that the best testimonials now are captured in and delivered in multimedia. So there's a place to use them in print. There's a place to use them spoken. There's a place to show them on video. Because this is all facts and figures and money and it's almost a B2B case history. Here's what we did and here's what the results are. Then you have what I call pictorial testimonials.
